summary: Mobile app with an 'Adaptive Orbit Dock' (Upload, Folders, Mission). Interaction behavior updated: swipe only changes active state visually (no auto-open); opening a function requires a tap on the centered (active) icon; tapping a side icon first rotates it to center (no open), then a second tap opens it. Tap feedback includes scale 0.95 for 100–120ms and light haptic feedback.
architectural_notes: Adaptive Orbit Dock has 3 items: Upload, Folders, Mission with infinite circular swipe, scale/opacity hierarchy, and orbital animation

existing_features.title: In the OrbitDock component (frontend/src/components/OrbitDock.tsx), remove any logic that automatically opens/activates a function when a swipe gesture lands an item in the center position. Swiping must only update the visual active state (the centered item) without triggering navigation or opening any view.

In OrbitDock, implement open-on-tap logic: a function (Upload, Folders, Mission) opens only when (a) its icon is already in the center/active position AND (b) the user taps that centered icon. A tap on the centered icon must fire the appropriate navigation/open callback.

In OrbitDock, implement rotation-first behavior for side (inactive) icon taps: when the user taps an icon that is NOT currently centered, the dock smoothly rotates to bring that icon to the center (active state), but does NOT open the function. A second tap on the now-centered icon is required to open it.

Add subtle tap visual and haptic feedback to all OrbitDock icon interactions: on every tap (whether on the centered icon or a side icon), the tapped icon scales down to 0.95 for 100–120ms before returning to its normal scale. Additionally, trigger a light haptic vibration (using navigator.vibrate([10]) or equivalent) if the browser/device supports it.

new_feature_requests.summary: Swipe on Orbit Dock only changes active state visually — does not auto-open function
Function opens only on tap of centered (active) icon; tap on side icon rotates to center first, requires second tap to open
Tap visual feedback: scale 0.95 for 100–120ms + light haptic feedback
